2021-10-01.title: North Korea Continues a Barrage of Missile Tests
2021-10-01.abstract: The North said it tested a new antiaircraft missile, the second missile test this week and the seventh this year, indicating that it is ramping up its weapons development.

2021-10-01.title: South Korea Shelves ‘Fake News’ Bill Amid International Outcry
2021-10-01.abstract: Right groups warned that a proposed new law would discourage the media from reporting critically on powerful people.

2021-10-01.title: A Princess Is Set to Be Wed. But It’s No Fairy Tale.
2021-10-01.abstract: Princess Mako of Japan will forgo the trappings of royal nuptials when she marries her college boyfriend, a commoner, after a long and arduous engagement.

2021-10-01.title: Why Diversity Seems Easier Said Than Done in Politics
2021-10-01.abstract: Minority members say they need to work twice as hard in a political system with “two sets of rules.”

2021-10-01.title: Australia accelerates its plans to allow international travel.
2021-10-01.abstract: Travel bans will begin to lift in November for citizens and permanent residents, depending on vaccination rates.

2021-10-02.title: Duterte Says He Will Retire Rather Than Seek the Vice Presidency
2021-10-02.abstract: The Philippine president, whose term ends next year, had been expected to seek the No. 2 office, but said he would not do so “in obedience to the will of the people.”

2021-10-02.title: Perilous, Roadless Jungle Becomes a Path of Desperate Hope
2021-10-02.abstract: The recent surge at the Mexican border is likely to grow as more migrants, mostly Haitian, risk everything negotiating the notorious Darién Gap on their way to the United States.

2021-10-02.title: As Bikers Throng the Streets, ‘It’s Like Paris Is in Anarchy’
2021-10-02.abstract: An ecologically minded experiment to make Paris a cycling capital of Europe has led to a million people now pedaling daily — and to rising tensions with pedestrians.

2021-10-02.title: U.K. Aims to Ease Trucker Shortage With Visas Into 2022
2021-10-02.abstract: The decision extended an earlier move to offer three-month visas to foreign drivers, and came as the government also called on military drivers to help resolve a fuel distribution crisis.

2021-10-02.title: Romans Choose From a Crowded Field to Run a Chaotic City
2021-10-02.abstract: Rome has defied efforts to make basic services work, and the incumbent mayor lags in the polls, with no shortage of rivals hoping to take her place.

2021-10-03.title: In a Surge of Military Flights, China Tests and Warns Taiwan
2021-10-03.abstract: For two straight days, Beijing sent a record number of planes near the island, Taiwan said, a display of strength that underscored Chinese demands for unification.

2021-10-03.title: 3,000 Yazidis Are Still Missing. Their Families Know Where Some of Them Are.
2021-10-03.abstract: Seven years after they were captured by the Islamic State, some members of the Iraqi religious minority are still being held captive by fighters in Syria. Others are with families of ISIS militants.

2021-10-03.title: Israel will require a booster shot to be considered fully vaccinated.
2021-10-03.abstract: The vaccination passports of more than a million people will soon be canceled if they don’t get a booster or show that they recently had Covid.

2021-10-03.title: They Helped Build Modern India but Are Shrinking as a People
2021-10-03.abstract: Parsis have supported many of the country’s institutions and nurtured business and the arts. But their numbers have dwindled at an alarming pace.

2021-10-03.title: Skateboards, Climate Change and Freedom: Germany’s Next-Generation Parliament
2021-10-03.abstract: A new generation of lawmakers is entering Germany’s Parliament. They felt ignored by the previous government, so they set out to change that by winning elections.

2021-10-04.title: In Mexico, Nearly 100,000 People Are Missing
2021-10-04.abstract: Nearly 100,000 people have disappeared in Mexico. Their families now search for clues among the dead.

2021-10-04.title: Eight Killed as Tensions Around India’s Farm Protests Worsen
2021-10-04.abstract: The police are investigating whether the son of one of Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s ministers was in a vehicle that slammed into protesters.

2021-10-04.title: London Police Announce Independent Review Amid Trust Crisis
2021-10-04.abstract: The move comes as the force faces scrutiny and criticism over its handling of violence against women, after the sentencing last week of Sarah Everard’s killer.

2021-10-04.title: Battling Delta, New Zealand Abandons Its Zero-Covid Ambitions
2021-10-04.abstract: The country is changing course seven weeks into a lockdown that has failed to end the outbreak and tested the patience of many residents.

2021-10-04.title: New Zealand abandons its goal of eliminating the coronavirus.
2021-10-04.abstract: Prime Minister Jacinda Ardern of New Zealand acknowledged an end on Monday to the country’s strategy of eliminating the coronavirus, announcing that restrictions would be gradually lifted in Auckland, the country’s largest city.

2021-10-05.title: Pandora Papers: A Money Bomb With Political Ripples
2021-10-05.abstract: Revelations from nearly 12 million leaked confidential financial records have thrown light on the concealed wealth of powerful public figures around the world. How do they hide their money, and why is this information important?

2021-10-05.title: The Best Movies and TV Shows New to Netflix, Amazon and Stan in Australia in October
2021-10-05.abstract: Our picks for October, including ‘Colin in Black & White,’ ‘Poltergeist’ and ‘Diana: The Musical’

2021-10-05.title: Russian Actress and Director to Start Making First Movie on Space Station
2021-10-05.abstract: The pair arrived at the International Space Station on Tuesday, aiming to shoot scenes for the first feature film made in orbit.

2021-10-05.title: Over 200,000 Minors Abused by Clergy in France Since 1950, Report Estimates
2021-10-05.abstract: An independent commission set up at the request of the Roman Catholic Church in France found that abuse was far more pervasive than previously thought.

2021-10-05.title: After Pastor Evicts Nearly 200 Migrants, His Brother Welcomes Them All
2021-10-05.abstract: An evangelical minister in a Mexico border town had let dozens of migrant families live in his church. When he ousted most of them, his brother across the way invited the outcasts into his home.

2021-10-06.title: Germany Sets Aside an Additional $767 Million for Holocaust Survivors, Officials Say
2021-10-06.abstract: Many of those now eligible for reparations are Russian Jews who survived the Nazi siege of Leningrad. They will receive annual pensions of about $5,200, or 4,500 euros.

2021-10-06.title: A Sea of White Faces in Australia’s ‘Party of Multiculturalism’
2021-10-06.abstract: A young daughter of Vietnamese refugees was set to run for Parliament. Then she was passed over for a white insider, and a debate over cultural diversity flared into the open.

2021-10-06.title: New Zealand will hold an ‘Election Day’-style vaccination event.
2021-10-06.abstract: The hope, for a single Saturday, amounts to vaccinating about one in 12 of those eligible for a shot, and one in 15 of the total population.

2021-10-06.title: In a building in Brooklyn, residents stopped paying rent. Thank the pandemic for their activism.
2021-10-06.abstract: Across New York City, many tenants who lost their jobs after the city went into lockdown are facing millions of dollars in unpaid rent. They’ve been kept in their homes by government aid programs and a state eviction moratorium that expires in January.

2021-10-06.title: Boris Johnson Pledges Transformed Economy for U.K.
2021-10-06.abstract: The prime minister sketched a vision of Britain on the cusp of change, but barely mentioned the spate of fuel and food shortages that have afflicted the country in recent weeks.

2021-10-07.title: Biden says vaccine mandates are the only way to defeat the virus.
2021-10-07.abstract: The president spoke in Chicago, where United Airlines recently announced a vaccine mandate for its 67,000 U.S. employees.

2021-10-07.title: Pakistan Earthquake Kills at Least 20
2021-10-07.abstract: The 5.9-magnitude quake struck Thursday morning while most people were asleep, and officials feared the toll may rise.

2021-10-07.title: The Afghan War: A Photographer’s Journal
2021-10-07.abstract: The Times photographer Tyler Hicks, who chronicled the 20-year war, captured American troops in battle, the deaths of civilians, schoolgirls in class and the struggles of ordinary Afghans to survive.

2021-10-07.title: Ndakasi, Gorilla Who Photobombed a Selfie, Dies in the Arms of Her Caretaker
2021-10-07.abstract: She died at age 14 in the Democratic Republic of Congo after “a prolonged illness,” said the park where she lived.

2021-10-07.title: Countries in the Asia-Pacific region rush to buy Merck’s Covid-19 pills.
2021-10-07.abstract: The home treatment, molnupiravir, is said by the company to potentially halve the risk of hospitalization and death from the virus.

2021-10-08.title: Covid’s Relentless Strain on a Melbourne Hospital
2021-10-08.abstract: From sadness to irreverent laughter, one emergency room doctor explains what it feels like to practice medicine in the epicenter of Australia’s latest outbreak.

2021-10-08.title: They Had the Vaccines and a Plan to Reopen. Instead They Got Cold Feet.
2021-10-08.abstract: Singapore’s experience offers lessons for other countries that want to reduce pandemic restrictions but have yet to deal with widespread infections.

2021-10-08.title: Potential Peace Prize winners are as varied as the issues facing humanity.
2021-10-08.abstract: In a year marked by climate change-driven disasters, political turmoil and the enduring devastation wrought by the coronavirus, the Norwegian Nobel Committee is choosing from a diverse array of 329 candidates for the Nobel Peace Prize.

2021-10-08.title: The Nobel winners for Chemistry helped reduce the environmental impact of molecules.
2021-10-08.abstract: The Nobel Prize in Chemistry was awarded on Wednesday to Benjamin List and David W.C. MacMillan for their development of a new tool to build molecules, work that has spurred advances in pharmaceutical research and lessened the impact of chemistry on the environment.

2021-10-08.title: The Tanzanian writer Abdulrazak Gurnah received the prize for Literature.
2021-10-08.abstract: The Nobel Prize in Literature was awarded on Thursday to Abdulrazak Gurnah for “his uncompromising and compassionate penetration of the effects of colonialism and the fate of the refugee in the gulf between cultures and continents.”

2021-10-09.title: Chicago’s mayor guts the city’s vaccine mandate for public workers.
2021-10-09.abstract: A day after President Biden visited to issue a national plea for more vaccine mandates, Mayor Lori Lightfoot said Chicago’s public workers could opt out of the city’s until the end of the year by getting regularly tested.

2021-10-09.title: As Johnson Draws a Happy Face, Britons Confront a Run of Bad News
2021-10-09.abstract: There’s a cognitive dissonance between Mr. Johnson’s upbeat appraisal of British life and the ills facing its citizens, including gas and food shortages and fears of rising energy prices.

2021-10-09.title: At Rwanda’s Favorite Bars, Forget the Beer: Milk Is What’s on Tap
2021-10-09.abstract: Milk is a favorite drink in Rwanda, and milk bars serve it up in abundance, fresh or fermented, hot or cold. “When you drink milk, you always have your head straight and your ideas right.”

2021-10-09.title: ‘Starting a Fire’: U.S. and China Enter Dangerous Territory Over Taiwan
2021-10-09.abstract: The self-ruled island has moved to the heart of deepening discord and rivalry between the two superpowers, with the potential to ignite military conflagration and reshape the regional order.

2021-10-09.title: Philippines’ Nobel Prize Newsroom Is Overjoyed but Under Siege
2021-10-09.abstract: Rappler, the news site co-founded by the new Nobel laureate Maria Ressa, dares to criticize the Philippine president, Rodrigo Duterte. He may yet see it shut down.

2021-10-10.title: Where Parents Have Abducted Their Own Children in a Bid for Custody
2021-10-10.abstract: For years, thousands of children in China have been taken and hidden. The practice rose along with the nation’s divorce rate. A new law aims to curb the practice.

2021-10-10.title: Pfizer will vaccinate entire city in Brazil as part of study.
2021-10-10.abstract: The study will follow participants for up to one year to find out how long vaccine protection lasts against Covid-19 and new variants.

2021-10-10.title: Electricity Is Restored in Lebanon, as Army Supplies Emergency Fuel
2021-10-10.abstract: It was a temporary reprieve for a grid that has been supplying only a few hours of electricity per day, as Lebanon’s collapsing economy struggles to import fuel.

2021-10-10.title: After Killings, U.K. Asks: Should Misogyny Be a Hate Crime?
2021-10-10.abstract: Prime Minister Boris Johnson says an emphatic no, but rights groups argue that it would add an extra layer of protection for women who face violence at the hands of men.

2021-10-10.title: Iraqis’ Frustration Over Broken Promises Keeps Voter Turnout Low
2021-10-10.abstract: Iraqis voted in parliamentary elections that were called a year early in response to anti-government protests.

2021-10-11.title: ‘This Drop Came So Quickly’: Shrinking Schools Add to Hong Kong Exodus
2021-10-11.abstract: The Chinese territory is experiencing its biggest population drop in decades as residents flee political repression and a new “patriotic” curriculum.

2021-10-11.title: Sydney roars back to life as pubs and stores open after three months.
2021-10-11.abstract: Australia’s largest city eased restrictions Monday after more than 100 days in lockdown.

2021-10-11.title: Missing Apostrophe in Facebook Post Lands a Man in Defamation Court
2021-10-11.abstract: An Australian man claimed a real estate agent did not pay its “employees” retirement funds. The lack of a punctuation mark may cost him thousands.

2021-10-11.title: Bashar al-Assad Steps In From the Cold, but Syria Is Still Shattered
2021-10-11.abstract: Arab countries are gradually restoring ties with Syria, but its president remains mired in crises he can’t escape.

2021-10-11.title: One in five of England’s most critically ill Covid patients are unvaccinated pregnant women, a study finds.
2021-10-11.abstract: Health officials say the risks from getting Covid-19 are far greater than any potential side effects from the vaccine.

2021-10-12.title: North Korea Displays Large Missile Arsenal Amid Stalled Talks
2021-10-12.abstract: The country’s leader, Kim Jong-un, says he doesn’t believe the Biden administration’s assertions that it has no hostile intent against his country.

2021-10-12.title: The Body Collector of Spain: When Migrants Die at Sea, He Gets Them Home
2021-10-12.abstract: Martín Zamora, the owner of a funeral parlor near Gibraltar, has found an unusual line of business among the relatives of those who drown trying to reach Europe.

2021-10-12.title: Countries around the globe turn to vaccine mandates to fight coronavirus.
2021-10-12.abstract: The number of activities available to the unvaccinated is shrinking, and the pressure to get shots is growing.

2021-10-12.title: Thailand proposes easing quarantine rules in a bid to lure New Year tourists.
2021-10-12.abstract: A new plan would lift travel restrictions for vaccinated travelers from 10 countries, including the U.S., provided they test negative for the virus.

2021-10-12.title: The New Jersey’s governor’s race puts mandates to a political test.
2021-10-12.abstract: New Jersey’s governor’s race, which is one of just two governor’s races in the country before next year’s midterm elections, is seen by some as an early barometer of voter sentiment.

2021-10-13.title: Amid U.S.-China Chill, Harvard Moves a Top Language Program to Taiwan
2021-10-13.abstract: A university spokesman said the move out of Beijing had long been planned. The program’s director cited a perceived lack of friendliness from the host institution.

2021-10-13.title: The U.S. Helped Build, Then Destroy, an Afghan School. We Went Inside.
2021-10-13.abstract: After years of fighting, Afghanistan’s war-torn rural areas are opening up for the first time. We traveled with the Taliban to a U.S.-funded school in Faryab Province that’s become a symbol of foreign intervention.

2021-10-13.title: This Pristine Beach Is One of Japan’s Last. Soon It Will Be Filled With Concrete.
2021-10-13.abstract: In rural Japan, the unstoppable forces of nature meet the immovable determination of the construction state. Can this village survive?

2021-10-13.title: Minnesota hospitals are nearing capacity as a virus surge batters the state.
2021-10-13.abstract: New daily cases have risen by 29 percent in the last two weeks and hospitalizations by 17 percent, according to a New York Times database.

2021-10-13.title: ‘Adapt or Die,’ U.K. Environment Agency Warns on Climate Change
2021-10-13.abstract: With more extreme weather events expected to bring floods and droughts, the body urged world leaders to focus on mitigating the effects of global warming.

2021-10-14.title: Hurricane Pamela Makes Landfall in Mexico
2021-10-14.abstract: The Category 1 hurricane came ashore northwest of the city of Mazatlán on Mexico’s west coast, downing trees, flooding streets and causing power outages. It was expected to weaken quickly as it moved inland.

2021-10-14.title: The Brooklyn Nets’ Kyrie Irving defends his decision not to get vaccinated.
2021-10-14.abstract: The N.B.A. star, who is set to make $40 million this year, said he opposes vaccine mandates, saying nobody should be “forced” to do it.

2021-10-14.title: Meteorite Crashes Through Ceiling and Lands on Woman’s Bed
2021-10-14.abstract: After a fireball streaked through the Canadian sky, Ruth Hamilton, of British Columbia, found a 2.8-pound rock the size of a large man’s fist near her pillow.

2021-10-14.title: Frida Kahlo, Aztec Gods: Can Art Lift Up a Poor Neighborhood?
2021-10-14.abstract: A new cableway and hundreds of giant murals have brightened lives in Iztapalapa, Mexico City’s most populous neighborhood, but poverty and attacks against women are still pervasive.

2021-10-14.title: At Least 46 Die as Blaze Tears Through ‘Ghost Building’ in Taiwan
2021-10-14.abstract: Dozens more were injured after a fire broke out at a 13-story building in the southern port city of Kaohsiung.

2021-10-15.title: Faulty Studs Led to Mexico City Metro Collapse, Attorney General Says
2021-10-15.abstract: A monthslong investigation by city authorities supports the central findings of a New York Times investigation, which had uncovered a series of fatal construction flaws.

2021-10-15.title: Man Whose Attack on Ex-Wife Was Livestreamed Gets Death Penalty in China
2021-10-15.abstract: The case riveted the public’s attention in China and spotlighted the patchy enforcement of the country’s law against domestic violence.

2021-10-15.title: Taiwan’s ‘Ghost Building’ Fire: a Death Trap for Dozens of Elderly
2021-10-15.abstract: “I had no choice but to live here,” said one resident who survived a blaze that killed 46 of her neighbors and raised questions about lax safety standards.

2021-10-15.title: ‘The Only Exit’: Where Soldiers Are Dying After Sexual Assaults
2021-10-15.abstract: The death of a woman in South Korea’s air force has rekindled outrage over the country’s armed forces, long criticized for abusing and discriminating​ against women.

2021-10-15.title: Australian residents stranded overseas will be allowed to return to New South Wales.
2021-10-15.abstract: New South Wales will become the first Australian state to allow fully vaccinated residents to return to the country without quarantining, starting next month, the authorities announced on Friday.

2021-10-16.title: Mining for Gold in Australia’s Migrant Past
2021-10-16.abstract: “New Gold Mountain” highlights the complications of belonging for immigrants then and now.

2021-10-16.title: New Zealand Attempts a Record-Setting ‘Vaxathon’
2021-10-16.abstract: The country took a different approach to encouraging vaccinations on Saturday, with barbecues, a telethon and the chance to get jabbed in the business-class seat of a Boeing 787.

2021-10-16.title: China’s Latest Craze: Scripted Murders, With Real Tears and Piracy
2021-10-16.abstract: “Scripted homicide” clubs have opened around the country as young people look for ways to escape and connect. Naturally, the government has questions.

2021-10-16.title: In the Land of the Godfather Comes a Ban on Them
2021-10-16.abstract: Part of the Catholic church in Sicily has imposed a three-year prohibition on naming godparents, arguing that the tradition has become merely a way to fortify family ties — and mob ties, too.

2021-10-16.title: What Are Political ‘Surgeries’ in Britain?
2021-10-16.abstract: The person-to-person meetings with voters give Britons unusually good access to their political representatives. But questions are being asked about whether the tradition can continue.

2021-10-17.title: Syria Accuses Israel of Assassinating Official Near Golan Heights
2021-10-17.abstract: Midhat Saleh, who oversaw the strategic Golan Heights boundary for Syria, was shot and killed by a sniper, the government said.

2021-10-17.title: Missionaries With an American Christian Group Kidnapped in Haiti
2021-10-17.abstract: Up to 17 missionaries and their family members were taken by gang members in the capital of a country where kidnappings have become increasingly common.

2021-10-17.title: South Korea Reconsiders a Rite of Manhood: The Draft
2021-10-17.abstract: Military conscription has become less popular. The rules have loosened. But experts still worry about the threat from the North.

2021-10-17.title: How a Stunning Lagoon in Spain Turned Into ‘Green Soup’
2021-10-17.abstract: Tons of dead fish have washed ashore in recent years from the Mar Menor, a once-crystalline lagoon on the Mediterranean coast that has become choked with algae. Farm pollution is mostly blamed.

2021-10-17.title: Tunisians Recall Revolution Reluctantly, if at All: ‘It Just Faded Away’
2021-10-17.abstract: When a monument to those killed in the 2011 uprising was recently damaged, few took notice or even cared in a town, and country, where there is now more regret than a wish to remember.

2021-10-18.title: For Missionary Group in a Fragile Haiti, Charity Turns to Chaos
2021-10-18.abstract: An organization based in Ohio and founded by Amish and Mennonites has been swept up in the violence roiling the country.

2021-10-18.title: Taiwan Couple Are Suspected of Negligent Homicide in Building Fire
2021-10-18.abstract: Prosecutors accused a woman of leaving a burning incense coil unattended in the building in Kaohsiung. Her partner was accused of failing to remind her to douse the coil before leaving.

2021-10-18.title: Colin Powell dies of complications from Covid.
2021-10-18.abstract: Colin L. Powell, who in four decades of public life served as the nation’s top soldier, diplomat and national security adviser, and whose speech at the United Nations in 2003 helped pave the way for the United States to go to war in Iraq, died on Monday. He was 84.

2021-10-18.title: Myanmar Announces Amnesty for Thousands of Anti-Coup Protesters
2021-10-18.abstract: The junta, which seized power in February, said it was releasing the prisoners to mark the Lighting Festival, a three-day holiday that begins Tuesday.

2021-10-18.title: Hello? This Is Colombia’s Antimachismo Hotline.
2021-10-18.abstract: A new hotline in Bogotá takes calls from men struggling with jealousy, control and fear — and challenges long-held assumptions about masculinity.

2021-10-19.title: North Korea Tests First Submarine-Launched Missile in Two Years
2021-10-19.abstract: The launch came after special envoys gathered to discuss how to deal with Pyongyang’s growing nuclear capabilities.

2021-10-19.title: Ecuador’s President Declares State of Emergency to Battle Crime
2021-10-19.abstract: Guillermo Lasso said the military and the police would patrol the streets to provide security to confront drug trafficking and other crimes.

2021-10-19.title: These U.S. Veterans Won’t Rest Until They’ve Kept a Wartime Promise
2021-10-19.abstract: An informal network that includes former government and military officials is working around the clock to fulfill a pledge to save Afghans who put their lives on the line for America.

2021-10-19.title: On a Pacific Island, Russia Tests Its Battle Plan for Climate Change
2021-10-19.abstract: President Vladimir V. Putin long dismissed the threat posed by global warming. But fires, disasters and foreign pressure have prompted him to change course.

2021-10-19.title: Reluctance among Russians to get vaccinated has led to a surge in cases and deaths.
2021-10-19.abstract: After Sofia Kravetskaya got vaccinated with Russia’s Sputnik V vaccine last December, she became a pariah on the Moscow playground where she takes her young daughter.

2021-10-20.title: Fox News anchor Neil Cavuto tests positive for coronavirus.
2021-10-20.abstract: The broadcaster has survived cancer and open-heart surgery and has multiple sclerosis.

2021-10-20.title: San Francisco temporarily closed an In-N-Out Burger for not checking customers’ vaccine status.
2021-10-20.abstract: The popular burger chain said it refused to become “the vaccination police for any government.”

2021-10-20.title: 1971: Paris Installs Giant Air Filters to Combat Pollution
2021-10-20.abstract: Two towers, each measuring 16 and a half feet high, were installed in an area in the east of the city in an experiment to tackle contamination.

2021-10-20.title: Afghan Women Who Once Presided Over Abuse Cases Now Fear for Their Lives
2021-10-20.abstract: They fear that they or their loved ones could be tracked down and killed because of their work delivering justice to women. “We have lost everything — our jobs, our homes, the way we lived.”

2021-10-20.title: Queen ‘Reluctantly’ Cancels Trip, Citing Medical Advice
2021-10-20.abstract: Britain’s 95-year-old monarch will not travel to Northern Ireland but is “in good spirits,” Buckingham Palace said.

2021-10-21.title: Blinken Cites ‘Democratic Reckoning’ in South America
2021-10-21.abstract: Secretary of State Antony Blinken warned of an eroding trust in democracy in the Western Hemisphere and described challenges posed to open government by authoritarian leaders.

2021-10-21.title: Searching for the Unmarked Graves of Indigenous Children
2021-10-21.abstract: For more than a century, Indigenous children in Canada were forced to attend residential schools, where many endured abuse. Thousands were never seen again and survivors were long ignored. We followed a team of archaeologists who came to the Muskowekwan First Nation to search for the graves of these lost children.

2021-10-21.title: India’s vaccine campaign hits a billion doses, and other news from around the world.
2021-10-21.abstract: More than 70 percent of India’s adult population has now received at least one dose of a vaccine. Bulgaria, with the E.U.’s lowest vaccination rate, faces a case surge.

2021-10-21.title: As Chinese Women Seek to Crack Male Professions, Schools Stand in the Way
2021-10-21.abstract: In China, some academic programs accept only men or cap the number of female applicants, who often must test higher than their male counterparts.

2021-10-21.title: In Australia, It’s ‘Long Live King Coal’
2021-10-21.abstract: The country has fallen behind other developed nations in its commitment to slashing carbon emissions. Neither fires nor international pressure has pushed it away from coal and other fossil fuels.

2021-10-22.title: Searching for Australia’s Climate Tipping Point
2021-10-22.abstract: The world, and most Australians, are increasingly disappointed by Australia’s lackluster approach to addressing climate change.

2021-10-22.title: New Zealand Wants a 90% Vaccination Rate. Its Street Gangs May Hold the Key.
2021-10-22.abstract: The country’s leaders have set aside some misgivings and cooperated with gang leaders to reach their communities.

2021-10-22.title: The U.S. starts giving Covid boosters to millions, as people in poor nations await their first doses.
2021-10-22.abstract: The booster program underlines the disparity in vaccine access, with wealthier nations accounting for more than three-quarters of all doses administered so far.

2021-10-22.title: Rambo, Romeo, Rome: His Posters Capture Films’ Essential Moments
2021-10-22.abstract: Renato Casaro’s hand-drawn art has hooked movie audiences around the world since the 1950s. Tarantino and Stallone are big fans. One secret to his success? “You can’t cheat.”

2021-10-22.title: How Russia Is Cashing In on Climate Change
2021-10-22.abstract: Global warming may pose grave dangers around the world, but as one tiny Russian town on the Arctic Ocean shows, it can also be a ticket to prosperity.

2021-10-23.title: For India and Pakistan, Cricket Is Never Just a Game
2021-10-23.abstract: It’s one of the greatest rivalries in sports, when war off the field allows it — a contest of national identities that will be renewed this Sunday in a World Cup match.

2021-10-23.title: She Is Breaking Glass Ceilings in Space, but Facing Sexism on Earth
2021-10-23.abstract: Sanitary pads and makeup: A Chinese astronaut’s six-month stay aboard the country’s space station has revealed conflicted cultural values toward gender.

2021-10-23.title: Turkish Opposition Begins Joining Ranks Against Erdogan
2021-10-23.abstract: With an eye on elections, six parties are working on a plan to end a powerful presidency and return to a parliamentary system.

2021-10-23.title: On the Scene: Needle Spiking in the U.K.
2021-10-23.abstract: We're covering the troubling reports of young women who say their drinks have been spiked on nights out.

2021-10-23.title: Struggles over vaccine mandates, the death of Colin Powell: the week in Covid news.
2021-10-23.abstract: As tens of millions of Americans remain unvaccinated, state and local governments have introduced mandates requiring public employees be fully inoculated against the coronavirus. And while orders in some localities have translated into a last minute surge in vaccination rates, they are also met with steadfast refusal, leading to legal challenges or concerns over staffing issues.

2021-10-24.title: As Hong Kong’s Civil Society Buckles, One Group Tries to Hold On
2021-10-24.abstract: Unions and other organizations have dissolved after facing pressure under a new security law. The Hong Kong Journalists Association is hoping it can avoid that fate.

2021-10-24.title: Please Don’t Feed the Whale Sharks? Fishing Town Says It Must, to Prosper.
2021-10-24.abstract: The chance to swim with the world’s biggest fish drew tourists to a Philippines town, but conservation groups denounce the hand-feeding that keeps the gentle creatures around.

2021-10-24.title: Long Arm of Russian Law Reaches Obscure Siberian Church
2021-10-24.abstract: The arrest of the leader of a small religious group reveals that Russian repression reaches even to the depths of the Siberian forest.

2021-10-24.title: I Was Hacked. The Spyware Used Against Me Makes Us All Vulnerable.
2021-10-24.abstract: Invasive hacking software sold to countries to fight terrorism is easily abused. Researchers say my phone was hacked twice, probably by Saudi Arabia.

2021-10-24.title: At the World Expo Pavilions, Future Visions Combine Past and Present
2021-10-24.abstract: Conversations with three figures integral to the conception of the expo.

2021-10-25.title: With New Conviction, Hong Kong Uses Security Law to Clamp Down on Speech
2021-10-25.abstract: An activist, Ma Chun-man, was convicted of inciting secession after, he said, he chanted slogans to test the limits of the law.

2021-10-25.title: Whose Promised Land? A Journey Into a Divided Israel
2021-10-25.abstract: Two Times journalists drive the length of Israel to discover what it means to be Israeli today. They meet a kaleidoscope of people, searching for belonging but far apart on how to find it.

2021-10-25.title: Troops opened fire on pro-democracy protesters who flooded the streets, with at least two reported killed.
2021-10-25.abstract: Protesters against the military coup took to the streets by the thousands and barricaded roads, and soldiers reportedly shot demonstrators outside army headquarters.

2021-10-25.title: Tensions between Sudan’s military and politicians grew in the past few months.
2021-10-25.abstract: Civilians were set to rule the country for the first time in more than three decades. But the military was concerned about losing power.

2021-10-25.title: Protesters Defy Military Coup in Sudan
2021-10-25.abstract: Pro-democracy protesters filled the streets of the capital, Khartoum, after the military detained the prime minister, suspended the government and declared a state of emergency in an apparent coup. At least three protesters were killed.

2021-10-26.title: Deadly Protests Follow Sudan Military Coup
2021-10-26.abstract: Sudanese soldiers fired on pro-democracy protesters who flooded the streets of the capital Khartoum after the nation’s top military commander dissolved the government and declared a state of emergency.

2021-10-26.title: At Last, a Royal Wedding. But No Trumpets, Just a News Conference.
2021-10-26.abstract: The torturous path to marriage for Princess Mako of Japan, who gave up her title to marry a commoner, leads to a registry office in Tokyo and most likely a life abroad.

2021-10-26.title: Australia Pledges ‘Net Zero’ Emissions by 2050. Its Plan Makes That Hard to Believe.
2021-10-26.abstract: The country’s last-minute commitment before next week’s climate summit is built on hope for new technology, and little else.

2021-10-26.title: Roh Tae-woo, 88, South Korean Leader in Move Toward Democracy, Dies
2021-10-26.abstract: He played a role in a bloody crackdown on pro-democracy protesters in 1980, then oversaw his country’s winning 1988 Olympics bid. He was later mired in legal trouble.

2021-10-26.title: As Gangs’ Power Grows, Haiti’s Police Are Outgunned and Underpaid
2021-10-26.abstract: The police force, dependent on an increasingly depleted state, is finding itself underfunded, underequipped and severely underpaid, even as it faces stronger opponents.

2021-10-27.title: Japan’s Communists Are Hardly Radical, but Make a Handy Election Target
2021-10-27.abstract: They have minimal support in polls. But by teaming up with other opposition parties for the first time, they have been made a boogeyman by the unpopular party in power.

2021-10-27.title: Gangs Rule Much of Haiti. For Many, It Means No Fuel, No Power, No Food.
2021-10-27.abstract: A country in crisis is facing a severe fuel shortage that is pushing it to the brink of collapse.

2021-10-27.title: Australians will soon be allowed to travel abroad freely if they are fully vaccinated.
2021-10-27.abstract: The eased restrictions will be the first stage in the country’s plan to reopen its borders since slamming them shut in 2020, separating families and leaving thousands stranded overseas.

2021-10-27.title: Taliban Allow Girls to Return to Some High Schools, but With Big Caveats
2021-10-27.abstract: In some provinces, teenage girls have been allowed to return to secondary schools, though some teachers and parents still have doubts about what this means about Taliban rule.

2021-10-28.title: Why China Is the World’s Last ‘Zero Covid’ Holdout
2021-10-28.abstract: The government has staked its political legitimacy on controlling the virus better than other countries, especially its geopolitical rivals.

2021-10-28.title: Meeting of Francis and Biden Will Highlight Rift With U.S. Bishops
2021-10-28.abstract: The pope and president, who will meet at the Vatican on Friday, are common targets of conservative American bishops seeking to undercut them.

2021-10-28.title: Daily coronavirus cases in Japan fall to the hundreds after a summer peak.
2021-10-28.abstract: Just a few months ago, the country was recording tens of thousands of cases daily. Now the numbers are in the hundreds each day.

2021-10-28.title: China plans to let vaccinated athletes skip quarantine at the Winter Olympics.
2021-10-28.abstract: Competitors will still face strict rules at what is shaping up to be the most extraordinarily regulated, large-scale sporting event since the pandemic’s start and could be expelled for breaking them.

2021-10-28.title: New Zealand will cut the amount of quarantine time for travelers in half.
2021-10-28.abstract: New Zealand’s government announced on Thursday that it would halve the mandatory quarantine period for travelers to the country next month.

2021-10-29.title: Nuclear-Powered Submarines for Australia? Maybe Not So Fast.
2021-10-29.abstract: Australia’s plan to build the submarines with U.S. and British help faces big hurdles. Supporters say they can be overcome. Critics say they may be too much.

2021-10-29.title: Saying Goodbye to Melbourne’s Long Lockdown (Again)
2021-10-29.abstract: It’s been a year and a day since the first time Melbourne reopened. Some things have changed. Some haven’t.

2021-10-29.title: The president’s meeting with Pope Francis was a diplomatic visit tinged with the personal.
2021-10-29.abstract: ROME — President Biden’s first day of a four-day diplomatic tour through Italy and Scotland includes meetings with European leaders who are skeptical about the durability of American democracy and will cover thorny topics ranging from diplomatic spats to cooperating on counterterrorism measures.

2021-10-29.title: As Biden and Francis meet, the U.S. Catholic Church remains divided.
2021-10-29.abstract: VATICAN CITY — Joseph R. Biden Jr. is the third American president Francis has met since becoming pope in 2013. Each meeting has marked a distinct phase not only of his papacy, but also of the political upheaval in the United States and in its Roman Catholic church.

2021-10-29.title: Children Drive Britain’s Longest-Running Covid Surge
2021-10-29.abstract: The United Kingdom lifted nearly all coronavirus restrictions in July — and has seen an extended wave ever since, with school-age children now making up more than a third of new cases.

2021-10-30.title: Ten Republican-led states sue over Biden’s vaccination mandate for federal contractors.
2021-10-30.abstract: The lawsuit is being led by Missouri’s attorney general, Eric Schmitt, and the attorney general of Nebraska, Doug Peterson.

2021-10-30.title: Protests Continue in Sudan
2021-10-30.abstract: Demonstrators took to the streets days after the military seized power in a coup on Sunday. They are demanding that the army chief, Lt. Gen. Abdel Fattah al-Burhan, step down and allow civilian rule.

2021-10-30.title: Protesters Killed in Sudan on Day Seen as a Test for the Military
2021-10-30.abstract: Three demonstrators were fatally shot, a doctors’ group said, as crowds answered activists’ call for a “march of millions” against a coup that has threatened the country’s democratic transition.

2021-10-30.title: Protesters march in Rome, chanting: ‘We are not the G20, we are change.’
2021-10-30.abstract: Several thousands protesters gathered in downtown Rome, representing a broad range of groups and causes: students and vaccine skeptics, labor union members and climate-change activists

2021-10-30.title: What is the G20?
2021-10-30.abstract: The Group of 20 aims to unite world leaders around shared economic, political and health challenges. It also serves as an all-purpose jamboree of diplomatic activity.

2021-10-31.title: Japan’s Election Was Closer Than Usual, Even Though Party in Power Has Hit Snooze
2021-10-31.abstract: The governing Liberal Democrats won on Sunday, despite having chosen a prime minister, Fumio Kishida, who’s known for his lack of charisma.

2021-10-31.title: U.K. Police Struggle to Curb Abuse of Women by Their Own Officers
2021-10-31.abstract: Sexual misconduct by police officers has prompted outrage across Britain and led to increasing skepticism that departments can investigate or discipline employees who abuse women.

2021-10-31.title: Rivals on World Stage, Russia and U.S. Quietly Seek Areas of Accord
2021-10-31.abstract: There have been a series of beneath-the-surface meetings between the two countries as the Biden administration applies a more sober approach to relations with the Kremlin.

2021-10-31.title: G20 leaders send a symbolic message on a key climate target.
2021-10-31.abstract: Countries will “pursue efforts” to limit global temperature rise to 1.5 degrees Celsius, compared with preindustrial times.

2021-10-31.title: Afghan Art Flourished for 20 Years. Can It Survive the New Taliban Regime?
2021-10-31.abstract: So far, the Taliban have not banned art outright. But many artists have fled Afghanistan, fearing for their work and their lives.
